desc: Gdańsk is a characterful Baltic port where Hanseatic facades, shipyard history, and beach-oriented Tri-City life combine into a flexible city-and-coast break. Demand rises from late spring through summer when waterfront events and seaside travel peak, while winter is calmer, windier, and better for museum-heavy itineraries.
hls: Hanseatic old town and Motława waterfront
Solidarity movement and shipyard heritage sites
Baltic beaches, piers, and coastal rail links
Strong amber craft and maritime food culture
Cooler summers than inland Polish city breaks

todo.name: Main Town heritage route
todo.desc: Link Long Market, Neptune Fountain, and St. Mary's for classic Gdańsk street scenery.

todo.name: European Solidarity Centre
todo.desc: Spend time on the modern exhibitions to understand the city's shipyard and labor-movement significance.

todo.name: Motława riverfront cruise
todo.desc: Take a short river cruise or island loop to view cranes, granaries, and modern waterfront redevelopment.

todo.name: Sopot pier half day
todo.desc: Use quick regional trains for beach time and boardwalk atmosphere in nearby Sopot.

todo.name: Westerplatte or WWII Museum
todo.desc: Choose one deeper wartime site to complement the old-town narrative.

todo.name: Amber and seafood evening
todo.desc: Pair amber workshops or galleries with Baltic fish dishes in the harbor area.

wifi.note: Generally reliable in city apartments and cafés; seafront promenades and beach stretches can have more variable mobile performance during peak weekends.

prac.fltNote: Direct flights are available from Gatwick; London City typically requires a connection. Return fares are often ~£35–£90 off-peak, ~£90–£160 in shoulder demand, and ~£160+ around summer holidays and Christmas market windows.
prac.visa: Poland is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally stay visa-free for up to 90 days in any rolling 180-day period.
alerts: Baltic wind can make shoulder-season temperatures feel noticeably colder than the forecast headline.
Sopot and waterfront areas get significantly busier on summer weekends — book central accommodation early.
Rain and wind can disrupt coastal boat trips even in warm months; keep backup indoor plans.

sweet: June to early September is best for beaches and long daylight; May and late September are quieter shoulder windows with decent weather.
